user_outcome: When sworn runs against any repo, its per-project runtime state (.sworn/ — sworn.db, supervisor-<release>.db) never appears in that repo's git status and can never be accidentally committed, because sworn self-ignores the directory it creates.
rationale: sworn writes a .sworn/ runtime dir (DB of dispatch/telemetry/supervisor state) into every repo it operates on, but never self-ignores it — confirmed: no .gitignore-writing logic exists, yet sworn's OWN repo gitignores .sworn/ (.gitignore line 26), so the engine assumes a courtesy it doesn't extend to consumer repos. On the live fired repo this surfaced as `?? .sworn/` in git status: untracked churning binary SQLite DBs. For an UNATTENDED run this is load-bearing, not cosmetic — a dirty worktree is a dominant loop-failure mode (project_coach_loop_worktree_hygiene), and any broad auto-stage could commit a binary DB into the consumer's release branch. The fix writes .sworn/.gitignore containing `*` (self-ignoring directory) at the point sworn creates .sworn/, so the dir vanishes from git's view with no change to the host repo's tracked files. Idempotent: never overwrite an existing .gitignore (an operator may have customised it). Best-effort: a write failure must not fail the run (this is a courtesy, not a verification gate).

effort_complexity.rationale: A few lines at the single .sworn/ creation site (internal/db/db.go MkdirAll) plus a test; no model dispatch, no cross-cutting change. The only subtlety is idempotency + best-effort error handling.

acceptance_criteria.text: When sworn creates the .sworn/ directory (the os.MkdirAll at internal/db/db.go where DefaultDir='.sworn' is materialised), it SHALL also write .sworn/.gitignore containing '*' so every file under .sworn/ (sworn.db, supervisor-<release>.db, and the .gitignore itself) is git-ignored. The fix SHALL cover both the run DB and the supervisor DB (verify both paths route through this creation site; if not, cover the other site too).
If .sworn/.gitignore already exists, sworn SHALL NOT overwrite or modify it (an operator-customised ignore is respected). Proven by a test asserting pre-existing content is preserved.

When sworn opens its DB inside a freshly-initialised git repo, `git status --porcelain` SHALL NOT list .sworn/ afterwards. Reachability artefact: a test that git-inits a temp repo, opens the sworn DB via the db package, and asserts .sworn/ is absent from porcelain status.

If the .sworn/.gitignore write fails (e.g. read-only filesystem), sworn SHALL NOT fail the run — the write is best-effort (a repo-hygiene courtesy, not a correctness gate). The DB open path's success SHALL NOT depend on the .gitignore write succeeding.
After the change, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./internal/db/...` SHALL pass (including the new self-ignore tests).
